# UC-10.18.6 · Broadcom Symantec SEP Unmanaged Endpoint Discovery vs Agent Heartbeat

## Description

Assets without a healthy SEP agent are blind spots for malware and policy enforcement. Comparing authoritative inventory to recent `symantec:ep:agent:file` heartbeats finds missing, broken, or decommissioned agents that still appear in CMDB.

## Value

Closes coverage gaps for endpoint protection SLAs and supports merger, BYOD, and contractor device governance programs.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `corporate_endpoints.csv` with canonical hostnames aligned to SEPM naming. (2) Schedule daily agent status ingestion. (3) Tune grace period for mobile users (for example three to seven days). (4) Auto-open tickets to desktop services. (5) Exclude lab VLANs via lookup.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup corporate_endpoints.csv
| rename hostname AS Computer_Name
| fields Computer_Name OU owner
| join type=left Computer_Name [search index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:agent:file" earliest=-7d
| eval Computer_Name=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| stats latest(_time) as last_sep_seen latest(Agent_Version) as sep_version by Computer_Name]
| eval days_since_sep=round((now()-last_sep_seen)/86400,2)
| where isnull(last_sep_seen) OR days_since_sep>3
| sort days_since_sep desc
```

## Visualization

Table (host, OU, owner, days since SEP), single-value (unmanaged count), bar chart by OU.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2772)
